context: Tests whether the agent correctly applies all merge preconditions (green CI, no requesting-changes reviews, no conflicts, up-to-date base), uses squash merge with Conventional Commit title, deletes head branches, recognises that Dependabot PRs require triage before babysitting, and flags the failed CI check as a blocker rather than ignoring it.

checklist.name: PR 301 merge decision
checklist.description: PR 301 is decided as 'merge' (all checks green, approved, clean, up to date)

checklist.name: PR 301 squash strategy
checklist.description: PR 301 merge entry specifies squash as the merge strategy (not merge commit or rebase)

checklist.name: PR 301 Conventional Commit title
checklist.description: PR 301 merge entry includes a commit title in Conventional Commit format (e.g. 'feat: ...' matching the PR type)

checklist.name: PR 301 branch deletion
checklist.description: PR 301 entry states the head branch should be deleted after merging

checklist.name: PR 301 label linkage noted
checklist.description: PR 301 entry acknowledges the ai:implement-spec label or SPEC-88 closure and notes that the linkage should be confirmed after merge

checklist.name: PR 302 blocked on CI
checklist.description: PR 302 is decided as 'blocked' because ci/test-integration is failing — not as 'merge'

checklist.name: PR 302 blocker described
checklist.description: PR 302 blocked entry names the failing check (ci/test-integration or integration tests) as the specific blocker

checklist.name: PR 303 Dependabot flag
checklist.description: PR 303 (Dependabot PR) is NOT decided as immediate merge — the agent notes it requires triage-dependabot first or flags it as a different workflow

checklist.name: No --admin flag mentioned
checklist.description: merge_decisions.md does NOT suggest using --admin flag to force-merge any of the PRs

checklist.name: All 3 PRs covered
checklist.description: merge_decisions.md contains a section for each of PR 301, 302, and 303
